4178.reason: [This loop condition can cause NullPointerException when NextToken(i) returns null, and the logic for detecting LVal is flawed as it doesn't properly follow the grammar rules for LVal which should only check for immediate assignment or array access]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This loop condition can cause NullPointerException when NextToken(i) returns null, and the logic for detecting LVal is flawed as it doesn't properly follow the grammar rules for LVal which should only check for immediate assignment or array access]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This loop condition assumes a semicolon will always be found, which may cause NullPointerException if the token stream ends unexpectedly or if there's no semicolon. Also, the logic for determining LVal is flawed because it doesn't properly handle array access syntax and may incorrectly return false when encountering valid LVal expressions with brackets.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This loop condition assumes there will always be a semicolon ahead, but may cause NullPointerException when NextToken(i) returns null for out-of-bounds access]

4190.reason: [This early return is incorrect - encountering a non-bracket token after an expression doesn't necessarily mean it's not an LVal. The method should continue scanning until it finds either '=' or ';'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This early return is incorrect - encountering a non-bracket token after an expression doesn't necessarily mean it's not an LVal. The method should continue scanning until it finds either '=' or ';'.]

2648.reason: [The AST traversal outputs syntax components in post-order, but the problem requires outputting syntax component names BEFORE completing analysis of their children (pre-order like behavior). This causes incorrect output order where terminal tokens appear before their parent syntax component names.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [The AST traversal outputs syntax components in post-order, but the problem requires outputting syntax component names BEFORE completing analysis of their children (pre-order like behavior). This causes incorrect output order where terminal tokens appear before their parent syntax component names.]

2905.reason: [When encountering a single '|', it should create a token for "|" not "||", otherwise the parser will see "||" instead of the actual single "|" character]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '|', it should create a token for "|" not "||", otherwise the parser will see "||" instead of the actual single "|" character]

2914.reason: [When encountering a single '&', it should create a token for "&" not "&&", otherwise the parser will see "&&" instead of the actual single "&" character]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', it should create a token for "&" not "&&", otherwise the parser will see "&&" instead of the actual single "&" character]
